touches.ai_summary: Owen Choi submitted an inbound demo request via website form, expressing interest in automating customer onboarding workflows. Mentioned current manual processes are creating bottlenecks as CS team scales.
next_steps: Rep to follow up within 24 hours to schedule discovery call

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent follow-up email acknowledging Owen's request and proposing three time slots for an initial discovery conversation. Included brief overview of how similar healthcare IT companies use the platform.
next_steps: Owen to confirm preferred time slot

touches.ai_summary: Owen confirmed Thursday 2pm slot and mentioned the CS team is currently evaluating a few solutions including Fireflies. Asked if rep could share a brief capabilities deck before the call.

questions_asked.question: Can you send over a capabilities overview before our call?
questions_asked.answer: Rep sent one-pager highlighting onboarding automation features and healthcare customer case studies

next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for Thursday 2pm

touches.ai_summary: Brief pre-call touch to confirm Owen received the materials and answer quick questions about integration capabilities with their existing helpdesk system.

questions_asked.question: Does this integrate with Zendesk?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, native integration available with two-way sync

next_steps: Proceed with scheduled discovery call

touches.ai_summary: Initial discovery call with Owen. Discussed current onboarding process: 462 employees, CS team of 18, manually walking new healthcare clients through platform setup. Taking 4-6 hours per client. Owen mentioned evaluating Fireflies but concerned about limited workflow customization.

questions_asked.question: What's the biggest pain point in your current onboarding process?
questions_asked.answer: Time-intensive manual steps and inconsistent experience across different CS reps

questions_asked.question: What's driving the evaluation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Planning to scale CS team by 30% in Q3, need automation in place before new hires ramp

questions_asked.question: How customizable are the automation workflows?
questions_asked.answer: Fully configurable with visual workflow builder and conditional logic

next_steps: Owen to share current process documentation
Rep to prepare tailored workflow examples

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up discovery session diving deeper into technical requirements. Owen shared their onboarding checklist and compliance requirements specific to healthcare IT. Discussed need for audit trails and data residency considerations.

questions_asked.question: Are there specific compliance requirements we need to account for?
questions_asked.answer: HIPAA compliance is essential, need SOC 2 Type II certification

questions_asked.question: Can we set up role-based automation triggers?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, supports role-based workflows with conditional branching

questions_asked.question: What's the implementation timeline typically look like?
questions_asked.answer: Standard implementation is 4-6 weeks with dedicated success manager

next_steps: Rep to provide compliance documentation
Owen to loop in Madison Lewis from account management team

touches.ai_summary: Owen circled back to discuss pricing structure. Mentioned he's comparing us to Fireflies which quoted lower per-seat pricing. Asked about volume discounts for 22 seats and whether consulting hours are included.

questions_asked.question: What does the consulting package include?
questions_asked.answer: 12 hours of workflow design consulting plus ongoing optimization support

objections: Fireflies quoted significantly lower per-seat cost, need to justify the price difference
next_steps: Rep to prepare pricing comparison showing total value including consulting
Owen to review internally

touches.ai_summary: Owen mentioned the team is leaning toward our solution due to workflow flexibility, but budget approval is still pending. No VP or C-level sponsor identified yet. He's working on building internal business case.

questions_asked.question: Who typically approves software purchases at this budget level?
questions_asked.answer: VP of Customer Success, but Owen has direct budget authority up to $15k

next_steps: Owen to present business case to VP
Rep offered to join that conversation if helpful

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent email with ROI calculator and customer case study from similar healthcare IT company showing 40% reduction in onboarding time and improved CSAT scores.
next_steps: Demo scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Owen confirmed demo attendance and asked if Madison Lewis could join as well since she'll be primary day-to-day user. Requested focus on specific use case: automating compliance checklist delivery.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the demo to show our specific compliance workflow?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, rep will build custom demo environment with healthcare compliance scenario

next_steps: Demo confirmed with Owen and Madison attending

touches.ai_summary: Product demo conducted with Owen and Madison. Walked through automated onboarding workflow, compliance documentation triggers, and reporting dashboard. Madison asked detailed questions about day-to-day usage. Both seemed engaged and positive about capabilities vs. Fireflies.

questions_asked.question: How difficult is it to modify workflows once they're built?
questions_asked.answer: Drag-and-drop interface, changes can be made in minutes without technical resources

questions_asked.question: Can we A/B test different onboarding approaches?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, built-in experiment framework with statistical significance tracking

questions_asked.question: What kind of training is included for end users like Madison?
questions_asked.answer: Live training sessions, video library, and dedicated Slack channel for first 90 days

next_steps: Owen to discuss next steps with VP
Rep to send follow-up with pricing proposal

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent post-demo recap email with demo recording, pricing proposal for 22 seats at $19,500 ACV including consulting package, and implementation timeline.
next_steps: Owen to review proposal and schedule follow-up call

touches.ai_summary: Owen responded that the team loves the product but the price point is challenging. Asked if there's flexibility on the consulting hours since they have internal resources that could handle some implementation work.

questions_asked.question: Can we reduce consulting hours to bring the price down?
questions_asked.answer: Rep explained consulting is part of the package tier, but can explore alternatives on upcoming call

next_steps: Proposal review call scheduled

touches.ai_summary: Proposal discussion call with Owen. He reiterated budget constraints and mentioned Fireflies is still in consideration at lower price point. Rep explained value differentiation but Owen needs to see movement on price to get VP approval.

questions_asked.question: What price point would work within your approved budget?
questions_asked.answer: Owen indicated $15k would be within his direct authority, anything above needs VP sign-off

objections: Price is above Owen's direct approval threshold, creating friction in internal process
next_steps: Rep to review discount options with sales leadership
Owen to continue building VP business case in parallel

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent revised proposal with 22.8% discount bringing total to $19,500 (close to the threshold but still requiring VP approval). Emphasized limited-time offer tied to quarterly close.
next_steps: Owen to present to VP with updated proposal

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Owen. He appreciated the discount but still navigating internal approval. VP hasn't been directly engaged yet. Owen mentioned timeline is slipping due to competing priorities and asked about contract flexibility on start date.

questions_asked.question: If we sign now, can we delay implementation start until Q3?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can accommodate delayed start date in contract terms

questions_asked.question: Would it be helpful to have a brief conversation with your VP to answer any questions directly?
questions_asked.answer: Owen said he'd prefer to present first and loop in rep if VP has questions

objections: Timeline slipping, other priorities emerging internally
next_steps: Owen to present to VP within one week
Rep standing by for VP questions

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent email checking in on VP conversation and offering to provide any additional materials that would be helpful for internal decision-making process.
next_steps: Awaiting update from Owen on VP discussion

touches.ai_summary: Scheduled executive alignment meeting with VP of Customer Success was rescheduled by Owen with 2 hours notice. Reason given was VP schedule conflict, but no immediate reschedule proposed.
next_steps: Owen to propose new time for VP meeting

touches.ai_summary: Owen called to discuss path forward. VP is still not deeply engaged but Owen has authority to move forward at the discounted price. He wants to proceed but asked for additional flexibility on payment terms (quarterly vs. annual).

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Rep explained standard terms are annual but can explore quarterly with finance approval

questions_asked.question: Is there a specific concern driving the payment terms request?
questions_asked.answer: Owen mentioned Q2 budget is tighter than expected, quarterly would ease cash flow

next_steps: Rep to check on payment term flexibility
Owen to confirm final decision timeline

touches.ai_summary: Final exec meeting (VP did not attend, Owen represented leadership). Agreed to move forward with annual payment at $19,500 for 22 seats. Owen confirmed Madison will be primary implementation contact. Contract sent for signature.

questions_asked.question: What does success look like for you in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: Automate top 3 onboarding workflows and reduce manual touch time by at least 30%

next_steps: Owen to review and sign contract
Kickoff call scheduled for following week
